name: Plan travel itineraries with AI using Apify, OpenAI, and Google Docs

parameters.formDescription: Plan your perfect trip! Enter your travel details below and we'll find the best flights, accommodations, restaurants, and attractions for you.

parameters.text:
=You are a travel expert. Please provide comprehensive travel recommendations for {{ $('Set Variables').item.json.destination }}.

Travel dates: {{ $('Set Variables').item.json.checkinDate }} to {{ $('Set Variables').item.json.checkoutDate }}
Number of travelers: {{ $('Set Variables').item.json.travelers }}

Please provide:

1. **TOP 10 RESTAURANTS** - Include a mix of local cuisine, fine dining, and casual options. For each restaurant provide:
   - Name
   - Type of cuisine
   - Price range ($, $$, $$$, $$$$)
   - Brief description
   - Must-try dish

2. **TOP 10 MONUMENTS & HISTORICAL SITES** - Include famous landmarks and hidden gems. For each provide:
   - Name
   - Brief history/description
   - Best time to visit
   - Estimated visit duration
   - Entry fee (if any)

3. **TOP 10 PLACES TO VISIT** - Include parks, neighborhoods, markets, viewpoints, etc. For each provide:
   - Name
   - Description
   - Best time to visit
   - Tips for visitors

4. **LOCAL TIPS** - Provide:
   - Best transportation options
   - Local customs to be aware of
   - Safety tips
   - Best neighborhoods to stay in
   - Common scams to avoid

5. **SUGGESTED ITINERARY** - Create a day-by-day suggested itinerary based on the travel dates.

Format your response in a clear, organized manner with proper sections and bullet points.

parameters.completionMessage:
=📄 View your document here:

{{ $json.documentUrl }}

Your personalized travel plan includes:
• Top 3 recommended hotels
• Available flights
• Restaurant recommendations
• Must-see monuments & attractions
• Local tips & suggested itinerary

Enjoy your trip! ✈️🌍

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!
### Collect travel details via a form, then scrape flights and hotels while AI generates recommendations — all compiled into a Google Doc.

Great for travel agencies, personal trip planning, or any service that needs to deliver a complete travel brief automatically.

### How it works
* User submits a travel form with airports, dates, and number of travelers.
* Three parallel tasks run: Booking.com hotel scraping, Google Flights scraping, and OpenAI recommendation generation.
* Results are merged, formatted, and written to a new Google Doc.
* The user receives a link to their personalized travel plan.

### Setup steps
* Create an [Apify](https://apify.com) account and add your API token as both an "Apify API" credential and an "HTTP Query Auth" credential (parameter name: `token`).
* Add your OpenAI API key as an "OpenAI" credential.
* Connect Google Docs via OAuth2 and update the `folderId` in the "Create Document" node.
* Activate the workflow and share the form URL.

### Requirements
* Apify account (for Booking.com and Google Flights scrapers)
* OpenAI API key
* Google account with Docs and Drive access

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord](https://discord.com/invite/XPKeKXeB7d) or ask in the [Forum](https://community.n8n.io/)!

Happy Hacking!

## 1. Trigger and input
The form collects departure/destination airports, dates, and traveler count. The Set node normalizes these into variables used by all downstream nodes.

## 2. Parallel data fetching
[Apify integration docs](https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/app-nodes/n8n-nodes-apify/)

Three tasks run in parallel: Booking.com scrapes top 5 hotels by review score, Google Flights scrapes the best flights, and OpenAI generates restaurant, attraction, and itinerary recommendations.

## 3. Merge and process
All three data streams are merged into a single item. The Code node structures hotels, flights, and AI recommendations into a clean object for the Google Doc.

## 4. Generate Google Doc and respond
[Google Docs node docs](https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/app-nodes/n8n-nodes-base.googledocs/)

A new Google Doc is created, populated with the formatted travel plan, and the form completion page returns the document link to the user.
